title: Kidambi Srikanth Loses US Open Final
indian_angle: India's Kidambi Srikanth reached the final of the US Open, showcasing his return to form on the international stage.
summary: Kidambi Srikanth's impressive run at the US Open came to an end in the final, where he was defeated by Su Li Yang.

title: Rounak Chouhan Stuns World No 6 Chou Tien Chen at US Open
indian_angle: Young Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan achieved a significant upset by defeating World No 6 Chou Tien Chen at the US Open.
summary: Rounak Chouhan, a self-proclaimed Cristiano Ronaldo fan, made headlines at the US Open by beating higher-ranked Chou Tien Chen in a thrilling encounter.

title: BAI Warns Against Unauthorized Badminton Leagues and Tournaments
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India has issued a stern advisory regarding unsanctioned badminton leagues and tournaments, urging players and officials to exercise caution.
summary: The BAI has released an advisory warning players, coaches, and officials about participating in or promoting unauthorized badminton events, emphasizing the importance of official sanctioning.

title: BAI Announces Selection Criteria for Asia U17 & U15 Junior Championships
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India has published the detailed selection criteria for the upcoming Badminton Asia U17 & U15 Junior Championships 2026, outlining the path for young Indian talents.
summary: Indian junior shuttlers aspiring for the Badminton Asia U17 & U15 Junior Championships now have clear guidelines following the release of the selection criteria and addendums by the BAI.

title: Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Tournament in Chennai Announced
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India has circulated details for the Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Badminton Tournament 2026, set to take place in Chennai.
summary: Chennai will host the Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Badminton Tournament from July 16th to 23rd, 2026, as per the latest circular from the BAI, attracting top national talent.

title: India Invites Expression of Interest to Host BWF World Championships 2026
indian_angle: India has formally invited Expressions of Interest to potentially host the prestigious BWF World Championships in 2026, a significant move for Indian badminton.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has released an Expression of Interest for cities interested in hosting the BWF World Championships 2026, signaling India's ambition to bring the global event home.
